text.title: Cross-Tool Migration Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding Cross-Tool Migration. Each pairs a binary check and a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every migration MUST pass through the six phases in order: discover, field-map, dry-run, cutover, verify, decommission. Phase skip is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Skipped phases (esp. dry-run) cause week-of cutover failures that cannot be rolled back.

rule.statement: Field map MUST be an explicit YAML artefact declaring source-field → target-field with type-coercion notes. Implicit defaults are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Implicit field mapping produces silent data loss; explicit YAML is reviewable and diff-able.

rule.statement: User identity merge MUST go through SSO (Entra/Okta/Google) mapping. Email-only matching is forbidden when SSO is available.
rule.rationale: Email-only matching breaks on email changes, aliases, and merged accounts; SSO mapping is canonical.

rule.statement: Cutover phase MUST have a documented rollback gate with a hard deadline. If verify fails before the deadline, rollback executes automatically.
rule.rationale: Without a pre-committed rollback, teams paper over verify failures and run on a broken system for weeks.

rule.statement: Source tool MUST NOT be decommissioned until verify phase passes with 100% issue parity and 30-day read-only soak.
rule.rationale: Premature decommissioning destroys the only rollback path; 30-day soak surfaces edge cases the dry-run missed.

rule.statement: If preconditions for Cross-Tool Migration fail (see Skip-If list in AGENTS.md), the operator MUST skip this methodology and document the fallback reason instead of fabricating an output.
rule.rationale: Applying Cross-Tool Migration outside its envelope produces false-precision artefacts. Explicit skip preserves epistemic discipline.
